title: Dam intro scene renders brighter than stock-ares at matched pose and registered framing (shore ROI luma 53.1 native vs 41.0 stock-ares)
suspect: unattributed: native-vs-N64-hardware shading/lighting on the Dam establishing shot
repro: SDL_AUDIODRIVER=dummy GE007_MUTE=1 GE007_ENABLE_LEVEL_INTRO=1 GE007_INTRO_CAMERA_INDEX=5 GE007_WINDOW_WIDTH=1440 GE007_WINDOW_HEIGHT=1080 build/ge007 --rom baserom.u.z64 --level dam --no-ui --deterministic --savedir <fresh> --screenshot-label X --screenshot-frame 60 --screenshot-exit ; compare ROI [80,95,480,80] against .../tier1/task0/stock/a_estab_pad273_stock_f1400.ppm. A 4:3 window is REQUIRED (FID-0135) or the ROI lands on different scene content.

evidence.path: src/game/lvl.c:1646 — Registered comparison (Task 5b): native 4:3 capture and stock-ares frame are pixel-registered at the IDENTITY transform (gradient-correlation fit sy=sx=1.000, ty=tx=0, corr 0.683), so framing is no longer a confound. Shore ROI [x=80,y=95,w=480,h=80] on the 640x480 canvas: stock-ares luma 41.0, blue-dominance B/(R+G+B) 0.465, B/max(R,G) 1.473; native (WebGPU, 4:3 window, registered) luma 53.1, 0.493, 1.582. Native is ~+12 luma (~+29%) and slightly bluer. GL agrees with WebGPU (whole-frame mean-abs-diff 2.283 = the documented DAM-R1a micro-deltas). Content-region mean-abs-diff native-vs-stock 21.23.

history.evidence: Registered comparison (Task 5b): native 4:3 capture and stock-ares frame are pixel-registered at the IDENTITY transform (gradient-correlation fit sy=sx=1.000, ty=tx=0, corr 0.683), so framing is no longer a confound. Shore ROI [x=80,y=95,w=480,h=80] on the 640x480 canvas: stock-ares luma 41.0, blue-dominance B/(R+G+B) 0.465, B/max(R,G) 1.473; native (WebGPU, 4:3 window, registered) luma 53.1, 0.493, 1.582. Native is ~+12 luma (~+29%) and slightly bluer. GL agrees with WebGPU (whole-frame mean-abs-diff 2.283 = the documented DAM-R1a micro-deltas). Content-region mean-abs-diff native-vs-stock 21.23.
history.note: Task 5b, requirement 4b. This observation was previously filed under 'DAM-R1' in docs/audit/DAM_RENDER_DEEP_DIVE_2026-07-18.md, but DAM-R1's established scope in that same document is WEB-vs-NATIVE backend divergence (DAM-R1a refuted, DAM-R1b refuted/closed for native, DAM-R1c a WGSL port gap) — none of which covers a native-vs-HARDWARE brightness gap. Tracked separately here. Note the prior Task-5 numbers (native 62 vs stock 41) were taken at an unregistered 16:9 capture, i.e. over different scene content; the registered gap is smaller (53.1 vs 41.0) but real. Not root-caused: candidates include the port's remaster post-FX defaults (tonemap/grade), fog/lighting evaluation, and ares' own VI/gamma emulation — none excluded yet.

history.evidence: Title corrected 'brighter than hardware' -> 'brighter than stock-ares': the reference capture is instrumented ares, and this entry's own notes list ares' VI/gamma emulation as an unexcluded candidate — real-hardware brightness is not established by the cited evidence.
